prerequisites: A Deputy account with access to a Deputy install (the account URL, e.g. https://{subdomain}.{geo}.deputy.com).
Permission to create OAuth clients / generate an access token in your Deputy install (the oauth_clients developer area).
Your Deputy install base URL, including subdomain and geographic region (geo), to set the base_url config field.

authMethods.name: Permanent token (API key)
authMethods.summary: Deputy authenticates API requests with a permanent token. Generate it in your Deputy install and supply it as the api_key field. The token is sent on each request as an Authorization: Bearer {token} header. Deputy shows the token only once when created, so store it securely. You can validate it with a GET request to {base_url}/api/v1/me, which returns the token owner.

setupSteps.title: Identify your Deputy install URL
setupSteps.body: Determine your Deputy account URL, which follows the pattern https://{subdomain}.{geo}.deputy.com (for example https://simonssambos.au.deputy.com). The API endpoint for that install is https://{subdomain}.{geo}.deputy.com/api/. Use this value for the base_url config field.

setupSteps.title: Create a permanent token
setupSteps.body: In your Deputy install, open the OAuth clients area at https://{install}.{geo}.deputy.com/exec/devapp/oauth_clients. Set up a Client ID, then on the Client ID page click 'Get an Access Token' to generate a permanent token. The token is shown only once in the modal dialog and cannot be recovered later, so copy and store it securely before closing the dialog.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Set base_url to your Deputy install API URL and set api_key to the permanent token you generated. Deputy passes this token as an Authorization: Bearer {token} header on every request.

setupSteps.title: Verify access
setupSteps.body: Confirm the token works by sending a GET request to {base_url}/api/v1/me. A successful response returns information about the token owner, confirming the connector can authenticate.
